query: recent studies on comorbidity of major depression and alcohol use disorder in adolescents leading to poorer mental health outcomes

retrieved_docs.title: Depression in Children -- Prognosis -- Curry et al investigated recovery and recurrence rates following treatment for adolescent MDD in the Treatment for Adolescents with Depression Study (TADS). The study reported that 88% of adolescents achieved recovery within 5 years; however, 46% experienced a recurrence. Risk factors for recurrence included greater baseline severity and residual symptoms after treatment. These findings highlight the chronic and recurrent nature of adolescent MDD and emphasize the need for long-term monitoring and sustained intervention. This study offers valuable insights into the course of adolescent depression and informs strategies to prevent relapse. [61]
retrieved_docs.content: Adolescents with chronic or recurrent depression are at increased risk for persistent mood disorders in adulthood. [47] In specialist psychiatry settings, patients with MDD have long-term recurrence rates between 50% and 64%. [56] Relapse rates are notably higher following partial remission (67.6%) compared to complete remission (15.18%). [57] Children and adolescents who experience recurrent or chronic depression that extends into adulthood are more likely to endure significant disability and impairment. [58]

retrieved_docs.title: Veteran and Military Mental Health Issues -- Prognosis -- Depression
retrieved_docs.content: MDD is variable, with less than half of cases resolving within 3 months of onset, compared to approximately 80% showing improvement within 12 months. MDDs falling under the severe specifier are associated with a higher risk of recurrence and can sometimes become a chronic condition requiring ongoing attention and maintenance. Studies have shown that patients who continued with pharmacotherapy for a minimum of 6 to 12 months following their first depressive episode experienced less risk of recurrence of symptoms than those who discontinued medication earlier (25% compared to 50%). Although remission is the ultimate goal, trials show that continued care and collaborative treatment programs reduce the prevalence and incidence of MDDs later in life. [88]

retrieved_docs.title: Drug and Alcohol Use (Archived) -- Etiology -- Co-occurring Disorders
retrieved_docs.content: There is a high rate of co-occurring mental disorders among adolescent youth (12 to 18 years) who use substances or have a substance use disorder, including: Conduct disorder Attention deficit hyperactivity disorder Major depressive disorder Bipolar disorder Anxiety disorder Schizophrenia Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) In studies of children 13 to 18 years of age with a mental disorder, rates of a co-occurring substance use disorder (SUD) have ranged from 61% to 88%.

retrieved_docs.title: Social Anxiety Disorder -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Comorbid psychiatric disorders occur in up to 90% of patients with SAD. SAD's presence is a predictor for the development of major depression and alcohol use disorder. Patients who have comorbid psychiatric disorders have an increased likelihood of greater severity of symptoms, treatment resistance, decreased functioning, and increased rates of suicide. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Recognizing Alcohol and Drug Impairment in the Workplace in Florida -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The misuse of substances over time also may result in the deterioration of the health professional's well-being. Use of stimulants, for example, may result in cardiovascular problems such as angina, hypertension, and myocardial infarction. Alcohol can lead to liver diseases such as cirrhosis. Patients suffer exposure to infectious diseases such as human immunodeficiency, hepatitis, and other blood-borne diseases. There are often coexisting mental disorders such as depression and anxiety; suicide can be an outcome for some. Impairment can lead to traumatic injuries such as falls, fractures, and head injuries. [11] [12] [13]

retrieved_docs.title: Major Depressive Disorder -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: MDD is one of the leading causes of disability worldwide. It not only causes a severe functional impairment but also adversely affects the interpersonal relationships, thus lowering the quality of life. Individuals with MDD are at a high risk of developing comorbid anxiety disorders and substance use disorders, which further increases their risk of suicide. Depression can aggravate medical comorbidities such as diabetes, hypertension, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, and coronary artery disease. Depressed individuals are at high risk of developing self-destructive behavior as a coping mechanism. MDD is often very debilitating if left untreated.

retrieved_docs.title: Veteran and Military Mental Health Issues -- Complications -- Substance Use Disorders
retrieved_docs.content: Psychiatric symptoms, including distress, often precede and exacerbate cravings in individuals with SUDs. The presence of comorbid psychiatric disorders can make SUDs more severe and challenging to treat. Among OEF/OIF veterans with SUDs, 82% to 93% had another co-occurring mental health disorder, and they were 3 to 4 times more likely to have PTSD or depression. Less than 1% of veterans had an isolated diagnosis of SUD. Veterans with dual diagnoses were more likely to experience homelessness and require disability benefits. They also tend to have lower quality of life, poorer relationships, and higher rates of aggression. [99] Interpersonal, legal, and professional consequences of alcohol use are twice as likely in binge drinkers compared to other drinkers (9% versus 4%). [17] Furthermore, those with SUDs have higher rates of medical comorbidities such as obesity, sleep disturbances, physical injury, and chronic pain. [109] Specific medical complications vary by the substance(s) used, routes of administration, and other factors, highlighting the need for thorough history-taking and a high index of suspicion.

retrieved_docs.title: Veteran and Military Mental Health Issues -- Prognosis -- Substance Use Disorders
retrieved_docs.content: A significant study on VA patient-centered medical homes found that depression, severe mental illness (excluding PTSD), and SUDs were associated with an increased 1-year risk of hospitalization and death. The authors attributed the exemption for PTSD to the higher frequency of healthcare encounters in patients with PTSD. This supports the idea that mental illnesses, including SUDs, are associated with poor outcomes but can be somewhat relieved by access to care. [94] In another study, veterans admitted into intensive PTSD programs were divided into 7 groups based on their substance use: no substance use, or use of alcohol, opiates, sedatives, cocaine, or marijuana alone, and use of multiple substances. Changes in non-substance use outcomes, including PTSD symptoms, violence, suicidality, medical problems, and employment before and after PTSD treatment, were compared, and the effect of abstinence on each group was evaluated. Although abstinence rates varied among the groups, it was consistently associated with improved outcomes, except for employment, in all the groups. [95]

retrieved_docs.title: Persistent Depressive Disorder -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Medication and psychotherapy combined may result in higher response rates and improved functioning.

retrieved_docs.title: Late-Life Depression -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Depression is associated with substantial disability. It often occurs in the presence of another mental disorder, and this co-morbidity leads to severe complications for the depressive episode. [20] Co-morbidity predicts more prolonged and more severe episodes of depression, increased risk of chronicity, interference with life and activities, hospitalization, and suicide attempts. It also interferes with the course of depressive disorder. It is an important factor in the assessment and treatment of a depressed patient. Common comorbid anxiety disorders are simple phobia, social phobia, generalized anxiety disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder, agoraphobia, panic disorder, substance-use disorders, alcohol dependence, and other drug dependence. [23] Another major complication of depression is thoughts of ending one’s life. Suicide rates are highest in the elderly—25% of all suicides are elderly. The probability increases in elderly white males; isolation further increases the risk. Common predictors of suicide risk include age, male separated widowed, isolated divorced, debilitating illness, substance abuse. Depression also increases the chances of medical co-morbidity in the elderly—cardiovascular disorders, [24] diabetes, dementia, stroke, stress ulcers, and, eventually, the development of cancer. [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Practice Issues in American Indians and Alaska Natives -- Epidemiology -- Psychiatric issues and Mental Health
retrieved_docs.content: Mental health disorders are amongst the top 10 reasons for IHS hospitalizations and outpatient visits. [14] A study exploring the epidemiology of psychiatric issues reported that AIAN men have a significantly higher incidence of a psychiatric disorder than NHW men (70.09% versus 62.1%). Similarly, AIAN women were also at a significantly higher risk of a psychiatric disorder than NHW women (62.9% versus 52.86%). The study also showed a higher incidence of 'any Axis I disorder,' 'any substance abuse disorder,' and 'any alcohol use disorder' amongst AIAN men and women compared to NHW men and women, respectively. Some of the important ones are listed here.

retrieved_docs.title: Persistent Depressive Disorder -- Pertinent Studies and Ongoing Trials
retrieved_docs.content: There are several areas for additional research: Further validation of the diagnosis of persistent depressive disorder Preferential use of medication, psychotherapy, or combination therapy Treatment effect modifiers such as family characteristics Effects of other psychotherapies, such as psychodynamic psychotherapy, attachment-based therapy, or family therapy Use of antidepressant medications or other psychotropic medications such as ketamine Use of interventional treatments such as transcranial magnetic stimulation or electroconvulsive therapy Use of lifestyle interventions such as exercise Effectiveness of treatments in minoritized populations Effectiveness of treatments in populations with developmental disorders Integrating mental health, medical treatment For example, results from one study found high rates of persistent depressive disorder and major depression among patients undergoing human immunodeficiency virus testing. [33] [34]

retrieved_docs.title: Alcohol and Substance Abuse Evaluation and Treatment in American Indians and Alaska Natives -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Included in the work-up and differential diagnosis of alcohol and substance use disorders are these co-occurring disorders, which can exist in the same patient independently but can mimic, as well as be exacerbated by substance and alcohol use disorders. These conditions include the following: Bipolar disorder Depression Panic disorder Generalized anxiety disorder Psychosis Mania Post-traumatic stress disorder Schizophrenia Schizoaffective disorders, bipolar or depressive type Stimulant usage is especially known to exacerbate or initiate psychosis, delusions, and hallucinations in patients with no previous history of mental health diagnoses.

retrieved_docs.title: Depression in Children -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Pediatric depression has a complex clinical course and is frequently associated with serious complications that impact long-term developmental outcomes. Affected children and adolescents often struggle with academic underachievement, social withdrawal, and strained family relationships, all of which contribute to impaired psychosocial development. [59] When left untreated, depression in youth significantly increases the risk of substance use disorders, self-harm, and suicidal behaviors—suicide is now one of the leading causes of death among adolescents worldwide. [60] Early-onset depressive disorders are associated with increased chronicity and a higher risk of recurrence in adulthood, highlighting a developmental continuity that emphasizes the need for early and sustained intervention. [61]

retrieved_docs.title: Alcohol-Related Psychosis -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Complications of ARP include: Depression Suicide Major psychosocial impairment Development of persistent hallucinations Development of schizophrenia-like condition

retrieved_docs.title: Recognizing Alcohol and Drug Impairment in the Workplace in Florida -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: When a health professional also develops a mental health issue such as depression, anxiety, schizophrenia, or severe personality disorder, the risk of impairment due to self-medication and treatment increases significantly. The rate of drug use by healthcare providers is thought to be as much as five times higher than the reference rate for nonmedical professionals. High stress specialties have the highest incidence of substance use problems. For example, emergency physicians experience substance use at a higher rate than most other physicians.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 2 -- Community 367
retrieved_docs.content: Alcohol and drug rehabilitation/detoxification is linked to متلازمة الامتناع عن تعاطي الكحول (alcohol abstinence syndrome), which in turn includes الذهان والاضطرابات الذهانية (SMQ) as a component of its clinical picture. الذهان والاضطرابات الذهانية (SMQ) comprises a broad spectrum of psychotic and affective conditions. It contains ذُهان الطفولة غير المحدد، حالة راهنة أو نشطة (unspecified childhood schizophrenia, active or current state), reflecting that disorders usually diagnosed in infancy have a broader relationship with early‑onset psychotic presentations. Within the SMQ group are several depressive entities: اضطراب اكتئابي أساسي، نوبة راجعة (major depressive disorder, recurrent episode), اضطراب اكتئابي أساسي، نوبة واحدة (major depressive disorder, single episode), اضطراب اكتئابي أساسي، نوبة واحدة في هدأة تامة (major depressive disorder, single episode in full remission), اكتئاب أَوبِيّ (obligatory depression), and التقلقل الوجداني (mood lability). Additionally, الذهان والاضطرابات الذهانية (SMQ) is associated with عاطفة متقلصة (blunted affect), indicating affective flattening as a characteristic feature of this syndrome cluster. Together, these relationships illustrate how substance‑use treatment, early‑life psychotic disorders, various depressive states, mood instability, and blunted affect are interconnected within the framework of the SMQ and alcohol abstinence syndrome, highlighting important considerations for comprehensive patient assessment and care.

retrieved_docs.content: (disorders caused by alcohol) is a subtype of اضطراب تعاطي المواد الإدمانية, illustrating the hierarchical relationship between alcohol‑specific disorders and broader addictive‑behavior diagnoses. Disorders typically diagnosed in infancy are related to ذُهان الطفولة غير المحدد، حالة راهنة أو نشطة, indicating that active unspecified childhood psychosis falls within the early‑life diagnostic spectrum. Collectively, these hierarchical classifications and shared symptomatology provide a comprehensive framework for assessing, diagnosing, and delivering targeted care across organic psychosis, depressive disorders, behavioral dysregulation, encephalitic conditions, and substance‑use disorders, while highlighting the clinical relevance of attention deficits, daytime sleepiness, and the overlap between organic psychosis and schizophrenic mania.

Substance‑use disorders (اضطراب تعاطي المواد الإدمانية) contain alcohol‑related disorders as a subset, and both are associated with the depressive spectrum and with behavioral dysregulation. The behavior disorder category also comprises non‑inflammatory brain disease (التهاب الدماغ غير المعدي (SMQ)), schizophrenia and other psychotic disorders (الذهان وال اضطرابات الذهانية (SMQ)), and delirium/non‑inflammatory brain pathology (الهذيان/الاعتلال الدماغي غير المعدي (SMQ)). Within the psychotic disorder group, depressive states (recurrent and single‑episode major depressive episodes, atypical depression, mood lability) frequently co‑occur, and psychotic disorders are linked to blunted affect (عاطفة متقلصة). Non‑inflammatory brain disease is associated with motor hyperactivity, muscular hyperactivity, excessive daytime sleepiness, and both transient and persistent disorders of wake‑initiation or maintenance, highlighting its role in motor and sleep regulation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2279
retrieved_docs.content: Depression (excluding suicide and self‑harm) (SMQ) is presented as a broad clinical category that includes several distinct subtypes and related conditions. Within this category, reactive depression (اكتئاب تفاعلي), endogenous depression (اكتئاب داخلي المنشأ), melancholic depression (اكتئاب سوداوي) and habituation (الاعتياد) are identified as specific forms of depressive illness. The same overarching depression concept also encompasses postpartum depression (excluding psychosis) (الاكتئاب التالي للولادة (باستثناء الذُهان)), emotional poverty (الفقر العاطفي), heightened vigilance (اليقظة), alcohol poisoning (تسمم كحولي), marijuana use (تعاطي المارجوانا), mood swings (تقلب الحالة المزاجية), memory impairment (خلل في الذاكرة), psychological distress (ضائقة نفسية) and loss of ambition (فقدان الطموح). By grouping these diverse depressive presentations, substance‑related effects, and psychosocial states under a single classification, clinicians can better recognize the varied manifestations of depression, understand their interrelationships, and tailor assessment and treatment strategies to address the full spectrum of depressive disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 1315
retrieved_docs.content: Citalopram, a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor commonly prescribed for depression, may also be employed in the therapeutic management of الاعتماد على الكحول (alcohol dependence) and تعاطي التَبغ (tobacco use). This suggests that citalopram can play a role in treating substance‑related disorders, offering clinicians an additional pharmacologic option when addressing patients who struggle with alcohol dependence or tobacco use. Integrating citalopram into treatment plans for these conditions can help improve overall outcomes by targeting both mood symptoms and addictive behaviors.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 79

Within the psychosis SMQ, mood‑related diagnoses such as recurrent and single‑episode major depressive episodes, melancholic depression (اكتئاب أَوبِيّ), mood lability, and mania (هوس شَبَقِيّ) are also members, highlighting overlap among depressive, manic and psychotic features. The non‑infectious encephalitis SMQ includes the two awakening‑related disorders, indicating that arousal disturbances may manifest as encephalitic processes. Non‑infectious encephalitis is characterized by hyperkinetic motor activity, increased muscular activity and pronounced early‑phase excessive daytime sleepiness. In the broader non‑infectious encephalopathy spectrum, apathy (اللامبالاة) is a component of the delirium/brain‑dysfunction syndrome (الهذيان/الاعتلال الدماغي غير المعدي (SMQ)), which also encompasses attention deficits, reduced

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 1 -- Community 47
retrieved_docs.content: Depression (excluding suicide and self‑harm) (SMQ) is presented as a broad clinical category that encompasses multiple depressive subtypes—including reactive depression, endogenous depression, melancholic depression, postpartum depression, and tolerance—as well as distinct sleep‑wake disturbances such as transient disorders of awakening initiation or maintenance and persistent disorders of awakening initiation or maintenance. The SMQ also subsumes a wide range of associated phenomena and comorbid conditions, including emotional poverty, indifference, heightened vigilance, alcohol poisoning, marijuana use, mood swings, attention disturbances, memory disturbances, psychological distress, poor concentration, motor hyperactivity, muscular hyperactivity, and excessive daytime sleepiness. Within this framework, loss of ambition is classified under depression, indicating that diminished motivation may manifest as depressive symptomatology. Non‑infectious encephalitis (SMQ) incorporates both the transient and persistent disorders of awakening initiation or maintenance, linking sleep‑wake regulation abnormalities to an encephalopathic condition. This SMQ further includes hyperkinetic motor activity, hyperactive muscle tone, and primary excessive daytime sleepiness, highlighting a spectrum of motor and arousal disturbances. Apathy is identified as a component of delirium/non‑infectious encephalopathy (SMQ), connecting reduced motivation with acute brain dysfunction. Delirium/non‑infectious encephalopathy also comprises attention disorder, reduced concentration, and primary excessive daytime sleepiness, underscoring the cognitive, attentional, and vigilance impairments that frequently accompany this syndrome. Together, these relationships illustrate the heterogeneous nature of depressive disorders and their overlap with sleep‑wake, motor, and encephalopathic abnormalities, guiding clinicians toward comprehensive assessment and tailored treatment strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 2 -- Community 662
retrieved_docs.content: Depression (excluding suicide and self‑harm) (SMQ) is presented as an overarching clinical category that encompasses a wide range of specific depressive subtypes, related conditions, and associated phenomena. Within this category, reactive depression (اكتئاب تفاعلي), endogenous depression (اكتئاب داخلي المنشأ), melancholic depression (اكتئاب سوداوي) and habituation (الاعتياد) are identified as distinct members, reflecting different etiological and symptomatic profiles. Post‑partum depression (الاكتئاب التالي للولادة (باستثناء الذُهان)) is also classified under the same SMQ, highlighting its relevance as a specific temporal form of depressive illness. Therapeutic interventions such as electroconvulsive therapy (العلاج بالتخليج الكهربي) are linked to the SMQ, indicating their use in managing severe depressive states. A variety of psychosocial and neurobehavioral factors are likewise incorporated, including emotional poverty (الفقر العاطفي), heightened vigilance (اليقظة), alcohol poisoning (تسمم كحولي), marijuana use (تعاطي المارجوانا), mood swings (تقلب الحالة المزاجية), memory impairment (خلل في الذاكرة), psychological distress (ضائقة نفسية) and loss of ambition (فقدان الطموح). Together, these entities illustrate the complex, multifactorial nature of depression as captured by the SMQ framework, emphasizing the importance of recognizing diverse presentations and contributing factors in clinical assessment and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 962
retrieved_docs.content: The category “disorders usually diagnosed in infancy” has a broader relationship with ذُهان الطفولة غير المحدد، حالة راهنة أو نشطة (unspecified childhood psychosis, current or active). This specific psychotic condition is a member of the larger classification الذهان والاضطرابات الذهانية (SMQ) (psychosis and psychotic disorders). Within the الذهان والاضطرابات الذهانية (SMQ) group, several mood‑related diagnoses are also listed as members: اضطراب اكتئابي أساسي، نوبة راجعة (major depressive disorder, recurrent episode), اضطراب اكتئابي أساسي، نوبة واحدة (major depressive disorder, single episode), اضطراب اكتئابي أساسي، نوبة واحدة في هدأة تامة (major depressive disorder, single episode in full remission), اكتئاب أَوبِيّ (atypical depression), and the symptom التقلقل الوجداني (mood lability). Moreover, the entire الذهان والاضطرابات الذهانية (SMQ) category is itself a member of عاطفة متقلصة (blunted affect). This network of relationships illustrates the overlap between early‑onset psychotic conditions, broader psychotic disorder classifications, depressive mood disorders, and affective symptoms such as blunted affect, emphasizing the need for integrated assessment and management in patient care.
